title: Whole-genome 3D architectural screen reveals modulators of brain DNA structure
authors: Parasar B et al. (Tan lab, Stanford)
abstract: Plate-C: high-throughput in-plate chromosome conformation capture profiling thousands of whole-genome architectures per day. Chemical screen of 2,956 samples / 834 conditions across 5 neuronal and glial types, plus 6,081 single-cell Easy Dip-C and 200,893 single-cell transcriptomes. Identified diverse, dose/time-dependent, cell type/species-specific 3D genome changes induced by HDAC, BET, mTOR, UPR, GSK3/Wnt, Hedgehog, cGAS/STING and neurotransmission perturbations. HDAC inhibition rewires brain genome within hours in newborn mice, inducing latent state orthogonal to normal differentiation.
